question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a Juju charm can observe, organized by category:

---

## Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Fired when Juju finishes provisioning the unit |
| `start` | Fired when a unit's initialization is complete |
| `config-changed` | Fired after install/upgrade and whenever configuration changes |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fired after the charm directory contents have been updated |
| `update-status` | Fired automatically at regular intervals (default: every 5 minutes) |
| `stop` | Fired when the controller is ready to destroy the unit |
| `remove` | Fired when the controller is ready to remove the unit completely |

---

## Relation Hooks
These use the relation endpoint name as a prefix (`<endpoint>`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | Fired when an application is related to another |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | Fired when a new unit joins an existing relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | Fired immediately after `relation-joined`, and when a related unit modifies its settings |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | Fired when a unit departs from an existing relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | Fired when the local unit is ready to depart the relation itself |

---

## Leadership Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | Fired when a unit is elected as the leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Fired when leadership changes or the leader modifies its settings |

---

## Storage Hooks
These use the storage name as a prefix (`<storage>`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | Fired when new storage becomes available to the charm |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Fired after the `stop` hook completes when storage is being removed |

---

## Secret Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | Fired when the secret owner publishes a new secret revision |
| `secret-rotate` | Fired when a secret becomes stale and should be rotated |
| `secret-expired` | Fired when a secret's expiry time is reached |
| `secret-remove` | Fired when a secret revision no longer has any consumers |

---

## Pebble / Workload Hooks *(Kubernetes charms only)*
These use the container name as a prefix (`<container>`):

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | Fired when the Pebble daemon in a container becomes ready |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | Fired when a Pebble notice of type `custom` occurs |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | Fired when a Pebble health check exceeds its failure threshold |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | Fired when a previously failing Pebble health check passes again |

---

## Upgrade Series Hooks *(Machine charms only)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before the OS series upgrade process begins |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after the OS series upgrade has completed |

---

**Total: 25 distinct hook events** (with several being parameterised by endpoint/storage/container/secret name, so the actual number of hooks dispatched at runtime depends on your charm's metadata).
